entries.name: docs
entries.description:
Source Documentation Extraction and Generation

This module provides comprehensive tools for extracting JSDoc/TSDoc comments
from TypeScript/JavaScript source files and automatically generating Markdown
documentation.

## Features

- **Automatic Extraction**: Parses JSDoc comments from functions, classes, interfaces, and types
- **Flexible Filtering**: Include/exclude patterns for selective documentation
- **Markdown Generation**: Converts extracted docs to organized Markdown files
- **Navigation Generation**: Auto-generates sidebar navigation metadata
- **GitHub Links**: Includes clickable links to source code on GitHub

## Supported JSDoc Tags

- `@param {type} name - description` - Function parameter documentation
- `@returns {type} description` - Return value documentation
- `@example` - Code examples (multi-line blocks)
- `@private` - Mark item as private (excluded from docs if private=false)
- `@default value` - Default parameter value
- Custom tags are preserved in the `tags` field

## Usage Flow

1. Call `extractDocs()` to parse source files
2. Call `generateMarkdown()` to create Markdown content
3. Call `writeDocs()` to write files to output directory
4. Generated nav.ts can be imported for sidebar navigation

entries.name: extractDocs
entries.description:
Extracts JSDoc documentation from source files in specified directories.

This function recursively searches directories for source files matching
the include/exclude patterns, then extracts all documented items (functions,
classes, interfaces, types) from those files.

## Process

1. **File Discovery**: Recursively walks directories, applying filters
2. **File Reading**: Loads each matching file's content
3. **JSDoc Extraction**: Parses JSDoc comments using the native parser
4. **Declaration Matching**: Pairs JSDoc comments with source declarations
5. **Result Collection**: Aggregates extracted documentation by file

## Include/Exclude Patterns

Patterns support:
- `**` - Match any directory structure
- `*` - Match any filename
- Standard glob patterns (e.g., `**\/*.test.ts`)

## Performance Considerations

- Uses filesystem I/O which can be slow for large codebases
- Consider using more specific include patterns to reduce file scanning
- Results are not cached; call once per build/dev session

entries.name: pm
entries.description:
Package Manager Tabs Plugin

Transforms <pm>npm install …</pm> blocks into a tab group with one tab per
package manager (npm/pnpm/yarn/bun). The single npm-style command is converted
to each package manager's equivalent natively in Rust (`transformPmEmbeds` in

tags.ox-content:
/napi), and the result reuses the same `ox-tabs` widget markup as
the generic `<tabs>` plugin so styling and keyboard navigation are consistent.

Syncing is opt-in (off by default): when enabled, the rendered group carries a
`data-ox-tab-group="pkg-manager"` attribute so the client runtime can keep
every package-manager group on the page in sync via localStorage.

Package-manager groups share the tab-group counter with the `<tabs>` plugin so
`data-group` ids (and the CSS produced by `generateTabsCSS`) stay unique.

entries.name: PmOptions
entries.description: Options for {@link transformPm}.

members.name: sync
members.description:
Enable opt-in synced package-manager tab groups. When `true`, a
`data-ox-tab-group="pkg-manager"` attribute is emitted so the client runtime
syncs the active package manager across every pm group on the page and
persists the choice in localStorage.

entries.name: tabs
entries.description:
Tabs Plugin - Pure CSS implementation

Transforms <Tabs>/<Tab> components into accessible HTML with CSS :has()
based tab switching (no JavaScript required).

The HTML rewrite is performed in Rust (`transformTabsEmbeds` in

tags.ox-content:
/napi), replacing the previous rehype parse/stringify round-trip.
The per-build group counter (consumed by `generateTabsCSS`) stays here and is
advanced by the number of groups the Rust transform reports, so CSS
generation still covers exactly the groups that were emitted.

entries.name: transform
entries.description:
Markdown Transformation Engine

This module handles the complete transformation pipeline for Markdown files,
converting raw Markdown content into JavaScript modules that can be imported
by web applications. The transformation process includes:

1. **Parsing**: Uses Rust-based parser via NAPI bindings for high performance
2. **Rendering**: Converts parsed AST to semantic HTML
3. **Enhancement**: Applies syntax highlighting, Mermaid diagram rendering, etc.
4. **Code Generation**: Generates JavaScript/TypeScript module code

The generated modules export:
- `html`: Rendered HTML content
- `frontmatter`: Parsed YAML metadata
- `toc`: Hierarchical table of contents
- `render`: Client-side render function for dynamic updates

entries.name: NapiBindings
entries.description:
NAPI bindings for Rust-based Markdown processing.

Provides access to compiled Rust functions for high-performance
Markdown parsing and rendering operations.

entries.name: SsgOptions
entries.description:
Static Site Generation options.

These options control the HTML files emitted at build time and the matching
dev-server preview behavior. Pass `false` to the top-level `ssg` option to
disable the whole SSG pipeline, or pass an object to customize the defaults.

members.name: enabled
members.description:
Enable the SSG pipeline.

Keep this enabled when ox-content owns page rendering. Disable it only when
another framework integration will consume the Markdown modules directly.

members.name: extension
members.description:
File extension used for generated routes.

The value should include the leading dot. For example, `.html` emits
`guide.html`, while an empty string can be used by custom deployments that
map extensionless output themselves.

members.name: clean
members.description:
Remove previously generated files from the output directory before writing
the new SSG result.

Leave this disabled when the output directory also contains assets produced
by other Vite plugins or external build steps.

members.name: bare
members.description:
Emit bare HTML with only the rendered Markdown body.

This skips the default navigation, layout shell, and theme styles. It is
mainly useful for benchmarking, fixture generation, or projects that wrap
the output in their own shell.

members.name: siteName
members.description:
Site name shown in the default theme header and title suffix.

When omitted, the renderer falls back to project metadata where available.

members.name: ogImage
members.description:
Static Open Graph image URL used for social sharing.

When `generateOgImage` is enabled, this value is still useful as a fallback
for pages that cannot produce a generated image.

members.name: generateOgImage
members.description:
Generate one Open Graph image per page.

Generated images are written alongside the SSG output and referenced from
each page's metadata. Configure rendering details with the top-level
`ogImageOptions` option.

members.name: lastUpdated
members.description: Add each page's last git commit timestamp to the default theme.

members.name: siteUrl
members.description:
Absolute site URL used when generating social metadata.

Set this when pages need absolute Open Graph image URLs. Include the origin
and any deployment base path, without a trailing page path.

members.name: theme
members.description:
Theme configuration for generated pages.

Use `defineTheme()` to build this object so custom theme modules and the
default theme extension points keep their expected shape.

members.name: navigation
members.description:
Sidebar navigation override.

When omitted, ox-content derives navigation from the Markdown file tree.
Provide this when migrating from systems such as VitePress where navigation
is intentionally hand-authored.

entries.name: ResolvedSsgOptions
entries.description: Resolved SSG options.

entries.name: OxContentOptions
entries.description:
Options for the core `oxContent()` Vite plugin.

The top-level options describe where content lives, which Markdown features
are enabled, and which build-time features should run. Feature toggles that
accept `boolean | Options` follow the same convention:

- `false` disables the feature.
- `true` enables the feature with its documented defaults.
- an object enables the feature and overrides only the provided fields.

members.name: srcDir
members.description:
Directory containing Markdown source files.

The path is resolved from the Vite project root. SSG, search indexing, and
dev-server routing all use this directory as the content root.

members.name: outDir
members.description:
Directory where generated files are written.

SSG HTML, search indexes, and generated assets are emitted under this
directory during production builds.

members.name: base
members.description:
Base path prepended to generated internal URLs.

Use this when the site is deployed below a sub-path, such as GitHub Pages or
a documentation route inside a larger application.

members.name: extensions
members.description:
Markdown-like file extensions to process.

Extensions are normalized with a leading dot and matched case-insensitively.
Add custom extensions when another authoring format is compiled to Markdown
before ox-content sees it.

members.name: ssg
members.description:
Static Site Generation options.

Passing `true` or omitting this option enables SSG with defaults. Passing
`false` disables the SSG plugin while still allowing Markdown module
transforms to run.

members.name: gfm
members.description: Enable GitHub Flavored Markdown extensions.

members.name: footnotes
members.description: Enable footnotes.

members.name: tables
members.description: Enable tables.

members.name: taskLists
members.description: Enable task lists.

members.name: strikethrough
members.description: Enable strikethrough.

members.name: highlight
members.description: Enable syntax highlighting for code blocks.

members.name: highlightTheme
members.description: Syntax highlighting theme.

members.name: highlightLangs
members.description:
Additional languages for syntax highlighting.
Accepts Shiki LanguageRegistration objects (e.g., TextMate grammars).
These are loaded alongside the built-in languages.

members.name: codeAnnotations
members.description:
Code block line annotations for fenced code blocks.

This feature is opt-in because it changes rendered code-block markup. Pass
`true` to enable ox-content's attribute syntax, or pass an options object to
change the meta key or enable VitePress-compatible notation.

members.name: wikiLinks
members.description:
Expand Obsidian-style `[[page]]` and `[[page|label]]` links.

Use this for knowledge-base style content where authors prefer short,
document-relative link syntax. Pass an object to override the base URL used
when resolving generated hrefs.

members.name: emojiShortcodes
members.description:
Expand `:shortcode:` emoji aliases to Unicode.

Built-in aliases cover common emoji names. Provide `custom` entries for
project-specific aliases or to override a built-in mapping.

members.name: attrs
members.description:
Enable markdown-it-attrs style `{#id .class key=value}` attributes.

Attribute blocks can be attached to headings, paragraphs, links, images, and
other supported Markdown nodes depending on parser context.

members.name: codeImports
members.description:
Import source snippets into fences with `<<< @/path/to/file.ts{region}`.

This is useful for documentation that must stay synchronized with examples
in the repository. Use `rootDir` when snippets should resolve from a
directory other than the Vite project root.

members.name: sanitize
members.description:
Sanitize rendered HTML with safe defaults or explicit allow lists.

Enable this for untrusted Markdown. The default allow lists are conservative;
pass an options object only when the content model intentionally needs extra
tags, attributes, or URL schemes.

members.name: editThisPage
members.description:
Append an "edit this page" link to rendered Markdown.

The feature is enabled only when `repoUrl` is provided in the options object.
Passing `true` keeps the feature disabled because there is not enough
repository information to generate valid links.

members.name: cjkEmphasis
members.description:
Recognize emphasis adjacent to CJK text. The native parser already supports
this behavior; the option documents the compatibility contract.

members.name: codeBlockLint
members.description:
Lint fenced code blocks during Markdown transforms.

Use this as a lightweight authoring check for missing languages or trailing
whitespace inside fences. For project-wide linting, prefer the exported
`lintCodeBlocks()` helper or the Markdown lint APIs.

members.name: codeBlockTypecheck
members.description:
Type-check TypeScript/TSX code fences via tsgo.

By default only fences with explicit opt-in metadata are checked. This keeps
incidental examples cheap while allowing docs-as-code snippets to fail the
build when configured with `mode: 'error'`.

members.name: docsTests
members.description:
Extract runnable fenced examples for Vitest docs-as-tests harnesses.

Collected examples can be written by the docs test helpers and executed as
part of a normal Vitest suite.

members.name: mermaid
members.description: Enable mermaid diagram rendering.

members.name: frontmatter
members.description: Parse YAML frontmatter.

members.name: toc
members.description: Generate table of contents.

members.description: Maximum heading depth for TOC.

members.description: Enable OG image generation.

OG image generation options.
Ignored unless `ogImage` or `ssg.generateOgImage` is enabled.

members.name: transformers

Custom AST transformers.

entries.name: WikiLinkOptions
entries.description:
Options for expanding Obsidian-style wiki links.

The transform accepts `[[target]]` and `[[target|label]]` syntax and rewrites
it to regular links before rendering. It is intentionally small: path
resolution is based on the configured base URL rather than a full backlink
graph.

members.name: baseUrl
members.description:
Base URL prepended to resolved wiki-link targets.

When omitted, the top-level `base` option is used.

entries.name: ResolvedWikiLinkOptions
entries.description: Resolved wiki-link transform options.

entries.name: EmojiShortcodeOptions
entries.description:
Options for expanding `:shortcode:` emoji aliases.

The transform replaces recognized shortcode tokens with their Unicode emoji
equivalents during Markdown transformation. Unknown shortcodes are left
untouched so colon-delimited text can still be used by other tools.

members.name: custom
members.description:
Custom shortcode map merged with the built-in emoji aliases.

Keys should omit the surrounding colons.

entries.name: ResolvedEmojiShortcodeOptions
entries.description: Resolved emoji-shortcode transform options.

entries.name: AttrsOptions
entries.description:
Options for markdown-it-attrs style attribute blocks.

Attribute blocks let authors attach IDs, classes, and key/value attributes to
nearby Markdown nodes with syntax such as `{#install .lead}`.

members.name: enabled
members.description:
Enable the attrs transform when an options object is supplied.

Set to `false` to keep the object shape while disabling the transform.
This is mainly useful for config merging where callers want to preserve a
stable object structure.

entries.name: ResolvedAttrsOptions
entries.description: Resolved attrs transform options.

entries.name: CodeImportOptions
entries.description:
Options for importing source snippets into code fences.

The transform resolves `<<<` imports before code highlighting and other
code-block features run. Imported snippets therefore behave like ordinary
fenced code in later stages.

members.name: rootDir
members.description:
Directory used to resolve `<<<` imports.

When omitted, imports resolve from the Vite project root and configured aliases.

entries.name: ResolvedCodeImportOptions
entries.description: Resolved code-import transform options.

entries.name: SanitizeOptions
entries.description:
Options for sanitizing rendered HTML.

Sanitization happens after Markdown is rendered to HTML. This makes it useful
for user-authored content, but consumers should avoid enabling extra tags or
schemes unless the rendered output explicitly requires them.

members.name: allowedTags
members.description:
Allowed HTML tag names. Omit to use the built-in safe tag allow list.

Provide a full replacement list, not a list of additions.

members.name: allowedAttributes
members.description:
Allowed HTML attribute names. Omit to use the built-in safe attribute allow list.

Provide a full replacement list, not a list of additions.

members.name: allowedUrlSchemes
members.description:
Allowed URL schemes for link-like attributes.

Omit to use the built-in safe scheme allow list.

entries.name: ResolvedSanitizeOptions
entries.description: Resolved sanitize transform options.

entries.name: EditThisPageOptions
entries.description:
Options for appending an "edit this page" link.

The generated link points at the source Markdown file rather than the emitted
HTML route. Configure `branch` and `rootDir` to match the repository layout
users should edit.

members.name: repoUrl
members.description:
Repository URL used to build edit links.

The transform is enabled only when this value is provided.

members.name: branch
members.description:
Branch used in generated edit links.

Use the branch that accepts documentation changes, not necessarily the
branch that produced the deployed site.

members.name: rootDir
members.description:
Source root inside the repository, used before the page path.

Set this when `srcDir` is nested in a package or docs workspace.

members.name: label
members.description:
Link text rendered in the page footer.

Keep this short; the default theme renders it as a compact footer action.

entries.name: ResolvedEditThisPageOptions
entries.description: Resolved edit-link transform options.

entries.name: CodeBlockLintOptions
entries.description:
Options for linting fenced code blocks during Markdown transforms.

These checks are intentionally local to each fence. They do not execute code
or parse a project graph, so they are safe to run during normal Markdown
transformation.

members.name: languages
members.description:
Languages to lint. Omit to lint every fenced block language.

Language names are compared case-insensitively.

members.name: requireLanguage
members.description:
Require every fenced code block to declare a language.

This is helpful for documentation sites where every example should be
highlighted and searchable by language.

members.name: trailingSpaces
members.description:
Report trailing whitespace inside fenced code blocks.

The check reports the exact line and column range inside the fence content.

members.name: mode
members.description:
Diagnostic severity for lint failures.

Use `'error'` when code-block lint failures should fail the build.

entries.name: ResolvedCodeBlockLintOptions
entries.description: Resolved code-block lint options.

entries.name: CodeBlockTypecheckOptions
entries.description:
Options for type-checking TypeScript and TSX fenced code blocks.

Type-checking writes matching snippets to a temporary directory and invokes
`tsgo`. It is best suited for concise examples that should stay synchronized
with the public TypeScript API.

members.name: languages
members.description:
Fence languages to type-check.

Language names are compared case-insensitively.

members.name: requireMeta
members.description:
Require an opt-in fence meta marker before type-checking.

When enabled, only fences with metadata such as `typecheck` or `twoslash`
are checked.

members.name: tsgoCommand
members.description:
Command used to run the TypeScript checker.

Override this for package-manager scripts or workspace-local binaries.

members.name: mode
members.description:
Diagnostic severity for type-check failures.

Use `'error'` to fail the Markdown transform on broken snippets.

entries.name: ResolvedCodeBlockTypecheckOptions
entries.description: Resolved code-block type-check options.

entries.name: DocsTestOptions
entries.description:
Options for extracting fenced examples into docs-as-tests fixtures.

The extractor collects code fences that can be written into test files and
executed by the exported docs test harness helpers.

members.name: languages
members.description:
Fence languages to collect as runnable examples.

Language names are compared case-insensitively.

members.name: requireMeta
members.description:
Require an opt-in fence meta marker before collecting an example.

When enabled, only fences marked with metadata such as `test`, `runnable`,
`vitest`, or `docs-test` are collected.

entries.name: ResolvedDocsTestOptions
entries.description: Resolved docs-as-tests extraction options.

entries.description: Supported code annotation syntaxes.

members.name: notation
members.description:
Annotation syntax to enable.

- `attribute`: custom attribute syntax like `annotate="highlight:1,3-4"`
- `vitepress`: VitePress-compatible syntax like `{1,3-4}` and `[!code warning]`
- `both`: enables both syntaxes

members.name: metaKey
members.description:
Attribute name read from the code fence meta string.

Example: `annotate="highlight:1,3-4;warning:6"`

members.name: defaultLineNumbers
members.description:
Enable line numbers for all code blocks by default.

In `vitepress` or `both` mode, fenced code blocks can override this with
`:line-numbers`, `:line-numbers=<start>`, or `:no-line-numbers`.

Path to a custom template file (.ts, .vue, .svelte, .tsx/.jsx).
- `.ts`: default-export a function `(props) => string`
- `.vue`: Vue SFC, rendered via SSR
- `.svelte`: Svelte SFC, rendered via SSR
- `.tsx`/`.jsx`: React Server Component, rendered via SSR
If not specified, the built-in default template is used.

Vue plugin to use for compiling `.vue` templates.
- `'vitejs'`: Use `@vue/compiler-sfc` (official, default)
- `'vizejs'`: Use `@vizejs/vite-plugin` (Rust-based)

entries.name: DocsOptions
entries.description:
Options for source documentation generation.

The generator extracts JSDoc/TSDoc comments from JavaScript and TypeScript
source files, normalizes the declarations, and writes Markdown plus optional
navigation metadata. The defaults are optimized for documenting a package's
public `src` tree without exposing private implementation details.

members.name: enabled
members.description:
Enable source documentation generation.

The top-level `docs` option is opt-out: omitting it enables docs generation
with defaults, while `docs: false` disables the docs plugin entirely.

members.name: src
members.description:
Source directories to scan for documentation.

Paths are resolved from the Vite project root before applying `include` and
`exclude` patterns.

members.name: out
members.description:
Output directory for generated documentation.

The path is resolved from the Vite project root. Markdown pages, `docs.json`,
and generated navigation metadata are written under this directory.

members.name: include
members.description:
Glob patterns for files to include.

Patterns are evaluated inside each `src` directory.

members.name: exclude
members.description:
Glob patterns for files to exclude.

Excludes run after `include` matching and should cover tests, generated
files, and implementation-only entry points.

members.name: entryPoints
members.description:
Public API entry points used to group re-exported docs.

When omitted, docs are generated from the discovered source files without
entry-point grouping.

Use entry points when a package exposes a smaller public surface than its
source tree. Re-exported declarations are grouped under the entry point that
exposes them.

members.name: format
members.description:
Output format.

`markdown` is the primary supported format. `json` and `html` are reserved
for consumers that want to post-process extracted documentation data.

members.name: private
members.description: Include private members in documentation.

members.name: internal
members.description: Include internal members in documentation.

members.name: toc
members.description:
Generate table of contents for each file.
Reserved for future use; current generated API pages do not emit this TOC.

members.name: groupBy
members.description: Group documentation by file or category.

members.name: githubUrl
members.description:
GitHub repository URL for source code links.

When provided, generated documentation includes links back to the source
declaration lines.

members.name: linkStyle
members.description:
Internal documentation link style.

Use `markdown` for generated `.md` targets and `clean` for route-style links
consumed by static-site frameworks.

members.name: basePath
members.description:
Route prefix used by generated documentation links and nav metadata.

Nav metadata falls back to `/api` when this is not set.

members.name: pathStrategy
members.description:
Generated Markdown output path strategy.

`flat` emits one page per source module or category. `typedoc` emits
TypeDoc-like module, kind, and symbol pages for larger API references.

members.name: renderStyle
members.description:
Rendering style for generated API Markdown.

- `'html'` (default): HTML-laced Markdown with collapsible entries, stat
  blocks and member tables (ox-content theme).
- `'markdown'`: pure Markdown (headings, tables, fenced code) with no raw
  HTML scaffolding, suitable for plain Markdown hosts such as VitePress.

members.name: indexFormat
members.description: Display format for index items.

members.name: parametersFormat
members.description: Display format for value and type parameters.

members.name: interfacePropertiesFormat
members.description: Display format for interface property groups.

members.name: classPropertiesFormat
members.description: Display format for class property groups.

members.name: typeAliasPropertiesFormat
members.description: Display format for type alias property groups.

members.name: enumMembersFormat
members.description: Display format for enum member groups.

members.name: propertyMembersFormat
members.description: Display format for property-owned object literal members.

members.name: typeDeclarationFormat
members.description: Display format for return type declaration members.

members.name: typeParameters
members.description:
Opt in to TSDoc-style type-parameter documentation.

When enabled, declaration type parameters (`<T extends C = D>`) are
extracted into a structured "Type Parameters" section and `@typeParam` /
`@template` tags are merged in (and removed from the generic tag list).
`@typeParam` is a TSDoc feature, so this is off by default (JSDoc semantics).

members.name: renderStats
members.description: Emit the stats summary line on generated index pages.

members.name: renderGeneratedBy
members.description: Emit the generated-by attribution on generated root index pages.

members.name: groupOrder
members.description:
TypeDoc-style group order for module index sections and nav groups.
Use `*` as the insertion point for unlisted groups.

members.name: sort
members.description:
TypeDoc-style sort strategies applied to entries and members.
Strategies run in order; later strategies break ties from earlier ones.

members.name: sortEntryPoints
members.description: Preserve caller-provided entry point order when false.

members.name: kindSortOrder
members.description: TypeDoc-style declaration kind ranking for module sections and nav groups.

members.name: singleEntryRoot
members.description:
Single-entry root handling for TypeDoc-style generated docs.

When set to `'flatten'`, a single TypeDoc entry point uses the root
`index.md` as its landing page and omits the extra module level from
generated nav metadata. Symbol page paths stay under the entry point.

members.name: generateNav
members.description: Generate navigation metadata file.

entries.name: ResolvedDocsOptions
entries.description: Resolved docs options with all defaults applied.

entries.name: DocEntry
entries.description:
A single documentation entry extracted from source.

Entries represent top-level declarations such as functions, classes,
interfaces, type aliases, enums, variables, and modules. Members of compound
declarations are stored in `members`.

members.name: name
members.description: Exported or declared symbol name.

members.name: kind
members.description: Normalized declaration kind used for grouping and rendering.

members.name: description
members.description: Main prose extracted from the leading JSDoc/TSDoc block.

members.name: params
members.description: Function, method, or constructor parameter documentation.

members.name: returns
members.description: Return value documentation for callable declarations.

members.name: throws
members.description: Exceptions/errors documented with `@throws` / `@exception`.

members.name: examples
members.description: Code examples collected from `@example` tags.

members.name: tags
members.description: Additional tags preserved by tag name after known tags are normalized.

members.name: private
members.description: True when the entry is marked private or matched by private filtering.

members.name: file
members.description: Source file path relative to the extraction root when available.

members.name: line
members.description: 1-based start line of the declaration in the source file.

members.name: endLine
members.description: 1-based end line of the declaration in the source file.

members.name: signature
members.description: Full declaration signature, when the renderer can extract one.

members.name: members
members.description: Members belonging to classes, interfaces, object types, and enums.

entries.name: DocMember
entries.description: A member belonging to a class, interface, type alias, or enum entry.

members.name: name
members.description: Member name as it appears in the containing declaration.

members.name: kind
members.description: Normalized member kind used for rendering and sorting.

members.name: description
members.description: Main prose extracted from the member's documentation comment.

members.name: signature
members.description: Full member signature, when available.

members.name: type
members.description: Rendered TypeScript type text for properties and enum members.

members.name: default
members.description: Default value extracted from syntax or `@default` tags.

members.name: params
members.description: Parameter documentation for methods and constructors.

members.name: returns
members.description: Return value documentation for methods and accessors.

members.name: throws
members.description: Exceptions/errors documented with `@throws` / `@exception`.

members.name: optional
members.description: True when the member is optional in the source declaration.

members.name: readonly
members.description: True when the member is declared readonly.

members.name: static
members.description: True when the member is static.

members.name: private
members.description: True when the member is marked private or matched by private filtering.

members.name: tags
members.description: Additional tags preserved by tag name after known tags are normalized.

members.name: line
members.description: 1-based start line of the member declaration.

members.name: endLine
members.description: 1-based end line of the member declaration.

entries.name: ParamDoc
entries.description: Parameter documentation.

members.name: name
members.description: Parameter name, including dotted names for destructured properties.

members.name: type
members.description: Rendered TypeScript type text.

members.name: description
members.description: Prose extracted from `@param` / `@arg` documentation.

members.name: optional
members.description: True when the parameter is optional.

members.name: default
members.description: Default value extracted from syntax or `@default` tags.

entries.name: ReturnDoc
entries.description: Return type documentation.

members.name: type
members.description: Rendered TypeScript type text for the return value.

members.name: description
members.description: Prose extracted from `@returns` / `@return` documentation.

entries.name: ThrowsDoc
entries.description: Exception/error documentation.

members.name: type
members.description: Rendered TypeScript type text for the thrown value, when documented.

members.name: description
members.description: Prose extracted from `@throws` / `@exception` documentation.

entries.name: ExtractedDocs
entries.description: Extracted documentation for a single file.

members.name: file
members.description: Source module or file identifier used by generated output.

members.name: description
members.description: Optional module-level description extracted from a file header comment.

members.name: sourcePath
members.description: Absolute source path, when available for source links and diagnostics.

members.name: examples
members.description: Module-level examples collected from a file header comment.

members.name: tags
members.description: Module-level tags preserved by tag name.

members.name: entries
members.description: Top-level documented declarations found in this module.

entries.name: DocsSummary
entries.description: Summary counts emitted with generated documentation data.

members.name: modules
members.description: Number of modules included in the generated payload.

members.name: entries
members.description: Number of top-level entries across all modules.

members.name: byKind
members.description: Entry counts grouped by normalized declaration kind.

members.name: params
members.description: Number of documented parameters.

members.name: returns
members.description: Number of documented return values.

members.name: examples
members.description: Number of collected examples.

members.name: deprecated
members.description: Number of entries or members marked with `@deprecated`.

entries.name: GeneratedDocsData
entries.description: Machine-readable payload emitted alongside generated docs.

members.name: version
members.description: Payload schema version. Increment when the JSON shape changes incompatibly.

members.name: generatedAt
members.description: ISO timestamp for the generation run.

members.name: summary
members.description: Aggregate counts useful for dashboards and generated index pages.

members.name: modules
members.description: Extracted documentation modules in render order.

entries.name: NavItem
entries.description: Navigation item for sidebar navigation.

members.name: title
members.description: Display title for the navigation item.

members.name: path
members.description: Path to the documentation page.

members.name: children
members.description: Child navigation items (optional).

entries.name: SearchOptions
entries.description:
Options for full-text search.

Search indexes are built from Markdown content at build time and loaded by
the client runtime from `search-index.json`. Pass `false` to the top-level
`search` option to disable both index generation and the virtual search
module.

members.name: enabled
members.description:
Enable search functionality.

Set this to `false` when config merging requires an object shape but search
should be disabled.

members.name: limit
members.description:
Maximum number of search results.

This controls client-side result truncation, not the number of documents in
the generated index.

members.name: prefix
members.description:
Enable prefix matching for autocomplete.

Prefix matching applies to the final query token, which keeps normal terms
precise while still supporting typeahead-style interactions.

members.name: placeholder
members.description:
Placeholder text for the search input.

This value is embedded in the virtual search module for UI consumers.

members.name: hotkey
members.description:
Keyboard shortcut to focus search (without modifier).

Use an empty string to let the UI opt out of registering a shortcut.

entries.name: ResolvedSearchOptions
entries.description: Resolved search options.

entries.name: SearchDocument
entries.description: Search document structure.

members.name: id
members.description: Stable document identifier used by the search index.

members.name: title
members.description: Human-readable document title.

members.name: url
members.description: URL returned to search consumers.

members.name: body
members.description: Plain-text body content used for scoring and snippets.

members.name: headings
members.description: Headings extracted from the document.

members.name: code
members.description: Code block text extracted from the document.

entries.name: SearchResult
entries.description: Search result structure.

members.name: id
members.description: Matching document identifier.

members.name: title
members.description: Matching document title.

members.name: url
members.description: URL to open when the result is selected.

members.name: score
members.description: Relevance score returned by the BM25 search engine.

members.name: matches
members.description: Query terms that matched the document.

members.name: snippet
members.description: Context snippet with highlighted terms when available.

members.name: scopes
members.description: Hierarchical scopes derived from the result URL or document id.

entries.name: ScopedSearchQuery
entries.description: Parsed search query with optional scope prefixes.

members.name: text
members.description: Query text after `@scope` prefixes have been removed.

members.name: scopes
members.description: Deduplicated lowercase scope prefixes requested by the query.

entries.name: LocaleConfig
entries.description:
Locale configuration.

Locales define the routing and display metadata used by the i18n plugin.

members.name: code
members.description: BCP 47 locale tag (e.g., 'en', 'ja', 'zh-Hans').

members.name: name
members.description: Display name for this locale (e.g., 'English', '日本語').

members.name: dir
members.description: Text direction for rendered pages.

entries.name: I18nOptions
entries.description:
i18n (internationalization) options.

i18n is opt-in because it changes routing and build-time validation. Set
`enabled: true` and configure at least `defaultLocale` / `locales` when the
same content tree should serve multiple languages.

members.name: enabled
members.description:
Enable i18n.

The resolver returns `false` unless this is explicitly set to `true`.

members.name: dir
members.description:
Path to i18n dictionary directory (relative to project root).

Dictionary files are watched in development and checked during builds when
`check` is enabled.

members.name: defaultLocale
members.description:
Default locale tag.

The default locale is added to `locales` automatically when omitted from the
list.

members.name: locales
members.description:
Available locales.

When omitted, ox-content creates a single locale from `defaultLocale`.

members.name: hideDefaultLocale
members.description:
Hide default locale prefix in URLs.

When true, `/page` serves the default locale and `/ja/page` serves Japanese.
When false, all locales get prefixed: `/en/page`, `/ja/page`.

members.name: check
members.description:
Run i18n checks during build.

Checks validate dictionary coverage and translation function usage when the
native i18n checker is available.

members.name: functionNames
members.description:
Translation function names to detect in source code.

Add framework-specific wrappers here so build-time checks can find all
translation keys.

entries.name: ResolvedI18nOptions
entries.description: Resolved i18n options with all defaults applied.

entries.name: convertVitePressSidebar
entries.description:
Converts a VitePress sidebar config into ox-content navigation groups.
Nested VitePress items are flattened into the nearest ox-content group.

entries.name: convertVitePressNav
entries.description:
Converts VitePress top navigation into ox-content sidebar groups.
This is used as a fallback when no explicit sidebar is defined.

entries.name: fromVitePressConfig

entries.name: generateVitePressMigrationConfig
entries.description:
Generates a TypeScript module exporting migrated ox-content options.

This is used by the migration CLI so users can inspect and edit the resulting
object instead of keeping a runtime dependency on their VitePress config.
entries.signature: export function generateVitePressMigrationConfig(config: VitePressConfig, overrides: OxContentOptions = {}, options: GenerateVitePressMigrationConfigOptions = {}): string

entries.description: Normalizes VitePress-specific frontmatter into ox-content's entry-page shape.
